title: II. PanSeq: Synthesis and construction of the physical mid-copy T7-phage display library
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ek50o","text":"This protocol describes the construction of a T7-phage display library using single-stranded DNA oligonucleotides designed to encode peptide tiles. The PanSeq phage library consists of modular pools comprised of 64 amino acid peptides that encompass the entire human proteome, including annotated isoforms (N=586K), all predicted single intron retention and exon skipping events (N=305K), and small open reading frames, repetitive elements, and human endogenous retroviral elements (N=52K). A separate module encodes a broad sampling of common human pathogens and a limited set of allergens (N=853K). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"djaps","text":"Key materials and reagents have been listed under the \"Materials\" tab for user convenience to ensure reproducibility. All other materials and reagents may be sourced from reputable suppliers providing molecular-biology grade reagents. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fuv0l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2pa2s","text":"Appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) is recommended at all times. Frequent glove changes during phage work reduce the risk of contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"56qfm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a7fmg","text":"The T7 bacteriophage is highly infectious to E. coli cultures. Therefore, the use of T7 bacteriophage must involve a high degree of attention to avoid the risk of surface contamination which may impact other E. coli based research in the lab. Fastidious and regular decontamination of work surfaces and instruments with a dilute-bleach solution (1 part bleach to 4 parts water) is essential to reducing contamination. Waste should be carefully packaged and removed from the lab space on a daily basis. The ideal environment for phage work would allow for complete isolation of phage related work in an isolated space away from all other bacterial work.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ehtf1","text":"This protocol involves the use of microorganisms, which requires registration with the user's Institutional Biosafety Committee (IBC) prior to initiating experimental work.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Based on the oligonucleotide pool synthesis yield and average molecular weight, reconstitute each tube in the required volume of PCR-grade water to a final concentration of 10 nM. Prepare aliquots of the library pools and store at -20°C. Note : the Agilent PanSeq library pools were synthesized at 10 pmol scale. Thus, reconstitution at 1 mL was performed.

The amplified oligonucleotide pool must be digested using EcoRI/HindIII restriction endonucleases for directional cloning and ligation into the recipient T7 phage vector arms. Perform restriction digestion of the dsDNA library pool as follows: A B 1 Component Volume (μL) 2 amplified dsDNA library pool (normalized to 100ng/μL) 10 3 NEB buffer r3.1 5 4 NEB EcoRI 1 5 NEB HindIII 1 6 PCR-grade water 33 7 Total volume 50 Table 4. Digest reaction mixture for amplified oligonucleotide pool Perform the restriction digest reaction in a thermal cycler at 37°C for 1 hr followed by termination of the enzymatic activity at 80°C for 15 minutes. Note : the restriction digest can be performed on a larger scale to increase the final product, depending on the downstream processing goal.
In vitro packaging of the cloned oligo pool libraries, using the T7 Select System, results in the initial phage population. Perform the in vitro packaging reaction as follows:
Confirm the concentration of each oligonucleotide pool using the Qubit ssDNA quantification kit.

Recall from the PanSeq design protocol that PanSeq oligonucleotides are compatible with PepSeq and PhIP-Seq. To prepare the oligonucleotides for PhIP-Seq cloning, the T7 cloning restriction endonuclease sequences are added to the oligonucleotide library pool by PCR amplification. Prepare a 100 pM dilution of the oligonucleotide library pools in PCR-grade water and prepare a set of 8 50 μL PCR amplification reactions per library pool as follows: A B C 1 Component Single reaction volume (uL) 8x reaction volume (uL) 2 0.1 nM library pool 1 8 3 5x Q5 Reaction Buffer 10 80 4 10 μM PD_LIB-F 1 8 5 10 μM PD_LIB-R 1 8 6 10 mM dNTP mix 1 8 7 Q5 DNA Polymerase 1 8 8 PCR-grade water 35 280 9 Total volume 50 400 Table 1. Amplification reaction setup. A B 1 PD_LIB-F GTGGTCAATACGGAATTCTGTCCTATACTTCCAAGGCGCA 2 PD_LIB-R CGACCTCGAGAAGCTTATCAAAGCCAAGACAGAGAGTCACC Table 2. Amplification primer sequences.
Perform Ampure XP bead-based isolation of the resulting dsDNA amplicons. For further reagent information, refer to the Ampure XP manual under the Description section.
Using Ampure XP beads, purify the restriction-digested dsDNA library pool as described above in Step 5. Elute the dsDNA in 25 μL of PCR-grade water.

The presence of truncated or artifactual amplicons in the library is undesirable and can be eliminated using a size selection method based on the resources available to the user. We used the PippinHT (Sage Science) system to perform size selection using the size range-appropriate gel cassette according to the manufacturer's protocol.

Ligation the dsDNA library pool into the T7 10-3b bacteriophage vector arms provided in the T7 Select Cloning Kit at a 3:1 (insert:vector) ratio as follows: A B 1 Component volume (μL) 2 digested dsDNA library pool (0.06 pmol/μL) 1 3 T7 Select Vector Arms (0.02 pmol/μL) 1 4 NEB 5x T4 DNA ligase buffer (10x stock diluted 1:1) 1 5 NEB T4 DNA ligase 1 6 PCR-grade water 1 7 Total volume 5 Table 5. Ligation reaction mixture. The required number of ligations should be based on the size of the input oligonucleotide pool. For the PanSeq library, perform 4 ligation reactions per library pool. Note : the user may wish to perform a single ligation reaction and perform the packaging reaction and phage titer estimate as described below prior to full commitment of resources.
Perform initial phage titer by top-agar with BLT5403 cells according to the "Plaque Assay" method in the T7 Select System user manual. The method is briefly described as follows:

Phage library amplification in liquid culture is used to scale the library. The phage are combined with BLT5403 cells and allowed to propagate until the culture is fully lysed. The resulting phage are precipitated using a polyethylene glycol and sodium chloride solution and resuspended in a smaller volume of phage storage buffer. Prepare the amplification of the phage as follows:

Repeat the phage titer for each amplified library pool as described above. The target for the phage titer of the amplified pools is >10 11 pfu/mL. This titer, for the PhIP-seq library pools, allows >500-fold representation of each oligonucleotide peptide tile in the biospecimen immunoprecipitation protocol. The phage library is ready for immunoprecipitation with antibody-containing biospecimens and is referred to as Rd0 (or round 0).

Amplify the library pools using the following thermal cycler parameters: A B C 1 initial denaturation: 98°C 2min 2 15 cycles: 98°C 15sec 3 65°C 15sec 4 72°C 30sec 5 Final extension: 72°C 2min 6 Final hold: 10°C HOLD Table 3. Thermal cycler settings Note : Do not store the amplification reaction at 4°C. Proceed to amplicon purification without delay immediately following amplification of the library pool. Amplicon size heterogeneity may result if the Q5 enzyme is not removed from the reaction.

Assess the quality of the amplified dsDNA library pool by gel electrophoresis or preferably a high resolution analytical method such as Agilent TapeStation. A single amplicon of the predicted size should be observed and absent of primer dimer artifacts. Note : This step is critical as the presence of truncations will impact the uniformity of the downstream library production. Optimization of annealing conditions and PCR buffer additives should be performed if amplicon uniformity is not achieved using standard reaction conditions. PanSeq oligo pool following PCR amplification using variable primer inputs. Note the excess primer dimer product at higher concentrations. The amplicon, at approximately 280 bp, is evident as a single amplification product in relation to the NEB 100 bp DNA ladder in the first lane.
Determine the concentration of each amplified dsDNA library pool using the Qubit high-sensitivity dsDNA quantification kit.

Repeat size and purity assessment on the Agilent TapeStation to confirm a uniform population (single band) of the correct amplicon size following size selection of the restriction-digested library amplicon.
Determine the concentration of each ligation-ready dsDNA library pool using the Qubit high-sensitivity dsDNA quantification kit.

Terminate the packaging reaction by adding 270 μL of Terrific Broth for a final volume of 300 μL. Add 20 μL chloroform and gently mix the reaction volume by inversion. Immediately proceed with initial titer determination.
The day prior to in vitro packaging, begin an overnight culture of BLT5403 cells provided in the T7 Select System kit in Terrific broth containing 100 μg/mL carbenicillin.

Plaques will become visible as distinct zones of clearing and may be enumerated for each plate. Based on the input volume for each plate and the number of plaques, the user can calculate the titer in plaque forming units per milliliter (pfu/mL) for each packaging reaction. T7 plaques developed using the top-agar method. Note the zones of clearing relative to the opaque background growth on the agar plate. Each plaque represents a unique T7 phage clone.

The end-goal for PhIP-seq is to produce an initial phage library with representation in excess of the input oligonucleotide library. Calculate the total phage present in the packaging reaction and use this value to determine the appropriate number of additional packaging reactions required. ( Note : The user can combine all of the same pool packaging reactions together and repeat the titer to ensure sufficient representation has been accomplished prior to amplifying the phage for storage.)
Pool the total amplification reaction volume into a single 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube
Add 1x volume of thoroughly resuspended Ampure XP beads.
Thoroughly mix by gentle pipetting.
Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes to allow for binding
Briefly spin the microcentrifuge tube in a low-speed table-top centrifuge.
Place the microcentrifuge tube on a compatible magnetic rack and allow the beads to pellet for 2-3 minutes.
Carefully remove the supernatant into a waste container.
Gently wash (first wash) the beads with 70% (v/v) ethanol using twice the volume of the input sample, paying careful attention to the bead pellet to avoid disruption and loss.
Carefully remove the supernatant into a waste container.
Gently wash (second wash) the beads with 70% (v/v) ethanol using twice the volume of the input sample, paying careful attention to the bead pellet to avoid disruption and loss.
After removing the second ethanol wash volume, allow the bead pellet to air dry for 5 minutes. Note : avoid over-drying as this may reduce the recovery of the dsDNA.

Resuspend the pellet in 100 μL PCR-grade water, allowing the dsDNA to elute for 5min with gentle agitation, followed by magnetic pulldown of the pellet. Transfer the eluted dsDNA to a new microcentrifuge tube. Note : The beads may slough away from the tube during transfer if the user attempts to recover the full volume. Therefore, we recommend only attempting to recover approximately 90% of the reconstitution volume.
Normalize the concentration to 100 ng/μL using PCR-grade water.
Thaw one packaging reagent vial on ice for each library pool to be packaged.
Add 5 μL of ligation reaction to each packaging reagent vial and ensure complete mixing by stirring using an attached pipette tip. Avoid aggressive mixing as the reaction components are sensitive.
Incubate the packaging reactions at room temperature for 2 hrs to allow initial phage synthesis.
Normalize the concentration to 10 ng/μL, which corresponds to approximately 0.06 pmol/μL for the PanSeq library pools.
Incubate the ligation reaction overnight at 16°C in a thermal cycler
On the day of the in vitro packaging, prepare a fresh BLT5403 subculture in M9TB media (see Materials section) and grow to an optical density (O.D.) of 1.0.
Once at an O.D. of 1.0, hold the culture on ice until ready to perform phage titer determination.

In a 50°C water bath, prepare 14mL Falcon culture tubes containing 5mL molten top-agar. One tube is required for each dilution point. It is useful to prepare dilutions over a 10-fold range from 1:10 3 - 1:10 8 to ensure coverage.
Prepare dilutions of the packaging reactions in 100 μL M9TB media in sterile microcentrifuge tubes.
For each packing reaction dilution, prepare a sterile microcentrifuge tube containing 250uL of the BLT5403 culture at O.D. 1.0.
Combine each phage dilution with a companion BLT5403 tube.

Immediately, transfer the cell:phage mixture into a tube of molten top-agar and pour the combined contents onto a dry pre-warmed LB-carbenicillin agar plate. Gently tilt the plate in a circular motion to fully distribute the top-agar mixture over the full surface of the plate.
Allow the plates to solidify for several minutes before inverting and incubating at 37°C for 4-6hrs or overnight at room temperature.

The volume of cells required for each amplification is determined by the phage titer. The infection ratio of phage to cells, or multiplicity of infection (MOI), should be targeted at 0.001 (1000 cells per phage). The calculation for the number of cells in culture is determined using the following equation: OD 600 x (2 x 10 8 cells/mL)/(0.5 OD 600 ) x (culture volume) = number of cells
The day before the amplification, grow the BLT5403 strain as described above. The day of the amplification, prepare an appropriate culture volume of BLT5403 in M9TB to an O.D. of between 0.5 and 1.0.
Infect the culture volume with the phage library pool and incubate at 37°C and 300 rpm shaking until visible lysis of the culture is observed (typically 2 - 3 hrs post-inoculation).
Add 1/10 volume of 5M sodium chloride solution to the lysate and centrifuge at 13,000x RCF for 30 min at 4°C to remove cellular debris.

Transfer the cleared lysate to new sterile centrifuge bottles and add Phage Precipitation Solution to 20% (v/v) of the final volume (e.g., 100 mL in 500 mL total volume). Store the phage bottles on ice for 2 hours to allow for the phage to precipitate. Alternatively, the bottle can be stored at 4°C overnight.
Pellet the phage by centrifugation at 13,000x RCF for 45min at 4C.
Decant the supernatant into a container with bleach to neutralize any residual phage in the solution.

Resuspend the phage in 1/20th of the initial lysate volume with Phage Extraction Buffer. Add sterile 50% (v/v) glycerol to a final concentration of 10% (v/v) and prepare aliquots for storage at -80°C.
